scope_description: Release review for v0.1.0..3a073c8 before publishing v0.1.1, without claiming Windows support

excluded.reason: blocked: real Windows Server 2022 runtime and second-user ACL proof has not passed

head: 3a073c8

reviewer_manifest.execution_note: Six-reviewer full panel executed in two capacity-limited parallel batches; synthesis waits for all six.

findings.title: Windows endpoint resolution depends on Unix HOME
findings.summary: Production Windows endpoint resolution can derive a relative pipe name that does not match upstream herdr when HOME is absent.
findings.current_behavior: default_socket_path reads HOME and falls back to a relative dot path on every platform.
findings.impact: Normal Windows launches may never connect to or recover the real herdr process.
findings.scenario: A Windows user has no HOME variable; the gateway opens a pipe derived from .\.config while herdr uses the native profile path.
findings.proposed_fix: Centralize an OS-specific logical endpoint root using the native Windows profile resolver and add HOME-absent Windows tests.

findings.title: Windows smoke cannot reach first-run assertions
findings.summary: The smoke deletes the roaming config and then launches with that missing file as an explicit config.
findings.current_behavior: The gateway treats explicit config as load-only and exits when it is absent.
findings.impact: The blocking Windows proof cannot turn green and never reaches token, path, or runtime assertions.
findings.scenario: CI deletes APPDATA state, passes --config to the deleted config.json, and times out waiting for the gateway.
findings.proposed_fix: Run the first-start case without --config or create the explicit config first; keep a separate explicit-config case.

findings.title: Release can publish Windows before its proof passes
findings.summary: The release matrix uploads a Windows artifact on tags without depending on the blocked Windows runtime proof.
findings.current_behavior: Windows is an unconditional release-matrix entry while the proof is only a separate CI job.
findings.impact: An unsupported artifact can be publicly distributed and mistaken for supported functionality.
findings.scenario: A tag publishes the Windows ZIP even when Windows CI was skipped or failed.
findings.proposed_fix: Remove Windows publication until proof passes, or gate a clearly labeled preview artifact on the pinned proof.

findings.title: Windows ZIP contains Linux-only lifecycle material
findings.summary: The release workflow copies Bash installer and systemd service files into every archive, including Windows.
findings.current_behavior: All targets share one Linux-shaped archive allowlist.
findings.impact: The Windows artifact violates the locked foreground-only ZIP contract and presents unusable lifecycle files.
findings.scenario: A Windows user extracts the archive and receives install.sh and a systemd unit as apparent supported entry points.
findings.proposed_fix: Use a Windows-specific archive allowlist and assert its contents before upload.

findings.title: CI executes mutable remote PowerShell without integrity verification
findings.summary: The Windows job pipes the current herdr.dev installer response directly into Invoke-Expression.
findings.current_behavior: Every CI run executes unauthenticated mutable remote script content.
findings.impact: A compromised or changed upstream endpoint can execute arbitrary code in CI and makes the proof irreproducible.
findings.scenario: The site or CDN serves modified PowerShell that reads checkout credentials or alters the proof.
findings.proposed_fix: Download an immutable versioned artifact, verify a committed checksum or signature, and execute only the verified artifact.

findings.title: Smoke can silently test an unrelated herdr agent
findings.summary: When the named smoke agent is absent the script falls back to the first agent.
findings.proposed_fix: Use a unique smoke name, wait specifically for it, and fail instead of falling back.

findings.title: Missing CLI option operands silently select state-mutating defaults
findings.summary: A trailing --config or --bind stores None and continues as normal default startup.
findings.proposed_fix: Reject missing operands with exit code 2 and test long and short forms.

findings.title: Windows smoke does not reject a permissive existing token
findings.summary: The live proof only covers a newly created protected token and its repeat use.
findings.proposed_fix: Pre-seed a broadly readable token and prove startup fails before serving, then retain the distinct-user denial check.

findings.title: Installer writes an unescaped filesystem path into JSON
findings.summary: Quotes or backslashes in a valid workspace path can produce invalid starter configuration.
findings.proposed_fix: Generate JSON with an encoder or robust escaping and test quotes and backslashes.

findings.title: Native folder lookup panics instead of returning an actionable error
findings.summary: Windows root accessors call expect on fallible Known Folder lookup.
findings.proposed_fix: Return and propagate Result from native-root-dependent paths.

findings.title: Release Windows runner does not match the pinned proof platform
findings.summary: Release uses windows-latest while evidence is scoped to Windows Server 2022.
findings.proposed_fix: Pin windows-2022 and record the resolved image in release provenance.

findings.title: Windows smoke bypasses the gateway subscription adapter
findings.summary: The smoke invokes herdr terminal observe directly instead of consuming the gateway path that calls SocketHerdr::subscribe.
findings.proposed_fix: Exercise the production gateway event/subscription surface and keep direct CLI observe only as diagnostics.

findings.title: Local herdr transactions can hang indefinitely after connect
findings.summary: Write, flush, and response reads have no end-to-end timeout after the local endpoint opens.
findings.proposed_fix: Bound the complete transaction and test an accepted but silent or partial-response peer.

findings.title: Token validation and read are vulnerable to pathname replacement
findings.summary: Protection is checked by path and the token is opened again later; existing parent directories are not validated or protected.
findings.proposed_fix: Open once with no-follow semantics, validate the same handle, read from it, and validate the parent directory.

findings.title: Windows ZIP contains Linux-only lifecycle material
findings.resolution: No Windows archive is built or published; the remaining packaging path is Unix-only.

findings.title: Windows endpoint resolution depends on Unix HOME
findings.resolution: Native Windows profile resolution now determines the logical endpoint root, with injected-profile regression coverage.

findings.title: Windows smoke cannot reach first-run assertions
findings.resolution: The first-run smoke omits an explicit missing config; explicit-config repeat behavior remains a separate case.

findings.title: CI executes mutable remote PowerShell without integrity verification
findings.resolution: CI downloads an immutable preview binary, verifies its pinned SHA-256, and passes that exact path to every smoke invocation.

findings.title: Release can publish Windows before its proof passes
findings.resolution: Windows was removed from the release matrix until native runtime proof is available.

gate4.reason: Five open P1 findings block release; fixes and delta re-review are required before UAT and release approval.

decision.summary: Release review found 5 P1 and 9 P2 findings. No release, tag, push, or publication is approved.
